annotations.label: Pentagram

anchor.snippet: A pentagram (sometimes known as a pentalpha, pentangle, or star pentagon) is a regular five-pointed star polygon

annotations.note: No definition of pentagram or star polygons is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pentagram as simplest regular star polygon

anchor.snippet: The pentagram is the simplest regular star polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of regular star polygons.

annotations.label: Counts of points and segments

anchor.snippet: The pentagram contains ten points (the five points of the star, and the five vertices of the inner pentagon) and fifteen line segments.

annotations.note: No pentagram object in Mathlib whose points/segments could be counted.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol and symmetry group

anchor.snippet: It is represented by the Schläfli symbol {5/2}. Like a regular pentagon, and a regular pentagon with a pentagram constructed inside it, the regular pentagram has as its symmetry group the dihedral group of order 10.

annotations.note: The dihedral group D₅ exists in Mathlib as `DihedralGroup 5`, but it is not connected to a pentagram symmetry group; Schläfli symbols are absent.

annotations.label: Pentagram as net of pentagonal pyramid

anchor.snippet: It can be seen as a net of a pentagonal pyramid although with isosceles triangles.

annotations.note: Neither pentagonal pyramids nor polyhedral nets are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Construction by connecting alternate vertices

anchor.snippet: The pentagram can be constructed by connecting alternate vertices of a pentagon

annotations.note: No formalization of pentagons or this construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Construction by stellation

anchor.snippet: It can also be constructed as a stellation of a pentagon, by extending the edges of a pentagon until the lines intersect.

annotations.note: Stellations of polygons are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Golden ratio in pentagram edges

anchor.snippet: Each intersection of edges sections the edges in the golden ratio: the ratio of the length of the edge to the longer segment

annotations.note: `Real.goldenRatio` (φ) is defined in Mathlib, but its geometric appearance in pentagram edge sections is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ten isosceles triangles in pentagram

anchor.snippet: The pentagram includes ten isosceles triangles : five acute and five obtuse isosceles triangles.

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the substructure of a pentagram into isosceles triangles.

annotations.label: Golden triangles and gnomon

anchor.snippet: The acute triangles are golden triangles . The obtuse isosceles triangle highlighted via the colored lines in the illustration is a golden gnomon

annotations.note: Golden triangles and golden gnomons are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isosceles triangle with 36° angle

anchor.snippet: in an isosceles triangle with one or two angles of 36°, the longer of the two side lengths is φ times that of the shorter of the two

annotations.note: This specific triangle-φ relationship is not in Mathlib, although φ itself is defined.

annotations.label: Spherical pentagram

anchor.snippet: A pentagram can be drawn as a star polygon on a sphere, composed of five great circle arcs, whose all internal angles are right angles.

annotations.note: Spherical pentagrams and spherical star polygons are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polyhedra incorporating pentagrams
annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize specific polyhedra with pentagrammic features.

annotations.label: Pentagrams in Schläfli–Hess 4-polytopes

anchor.snippet: All ten 4-dimensional Schläfli–Hess 4-polytopes have either pentagrammic faces or vertex figure elements.

annotations.note: The Schläfli–Hess 4-polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pentagram of Venus

anchor.snippet: The pentagram of Venus is the apparent path of the planet Venus as observed from Earth

annotations.note: Mathlib has no astronomy content describing Venus's apparent path.

annotations.label: Venus 13:8 orbital resonance

anchor.snippet: Successive inferior conjunctions of Venus repeat with an orbital resonance of approximately 13:8

annotations.note: Orbital resonances and planetary motion are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ratio of longer to shorter segment equals φ

anchor.snippet: the ratio of the length of the edge to the longer segment is

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Real.goldenRatio = (1 + √5) / 2` and proves algebraic identities about it, but no statement equates it to a pentagram edge-section ratio.

annotations.label: Common ratio in isosceles subtriangles

anchor.snippet: In all of them, the ratio of the longer side to the shorter side is

annotations.note: The φ ratio between sides of the acute and obtuse isosceles subtriangles of a pentagram is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Napier's rules for right spherical triangles

anchor.snippet: rules that link the values of trigonometric functions of five parts of a right spherical triangle (two angles and three sides)

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize spherical trigonometry or Napier's rules for right spherical triangles.
